title: Hello planisphere
text: Earth sphere (precisely, geoid), three-dimensional object floating space. Yet maps use every day flat: exist two dimensions, paper screen. move curved reality flat representation, need transform coordinates expressed globe latitude longitude Cartesian coordinates plane. simple flattening operation: relies mathematical transformation known map projection, converts curved surface Earth flat map. Projections generally classified according geometric surface used intermediate step : cylindrical, conic, azimuthal, offering different way “wrapping” globe unfolding . However, transformation inevitably introduces distortions. way, similar trying press peel orange onto flat table. matter carefully proceed, surface lie flat without consequences: stretch places, compress others, may even tear fold forced. fundamental constraint applies Earth’s surface projected onto plane. projection can preserve geometric properties , meaning distances, areas, shapes, directions always altered degree, depending chosen projection purpose.

title: Hello planisphere
text: Earth perfect sphere; geodesy accurately approximated ellipsoid. refinement accounts slight flattening poles provides mathematical foundation high-precision geospatial computations. However, planisphere package, adopt spherical model Earth simplicity consistency visual geometric goals. need work directly ellipsoidal models geodesic accuracy, tools GDAL PROJ appropriate, operate ellipsoidal Earth representations.

title: Hello planisphere
text: hood, package relies set JavaScript libraries D3 ecosystem. First, d3-geo provides fundamental building blocks spherical geometry, including coordinate transforms set standard map projections. d3-geo-projection extends system wider collection projection methods, including discontinuous projections. Finally, d3-geo-polygon focuses spherical polygon operations: introduces additional projections rely polygon clipping. https://d3js.org/d3-geo/projection https://github.com/d3/d3-geo-projection https://github.com/d3/d3-geo-polygon

title: Hello planisphere
code: # install.packages("remotes") # remotes::install_github("riatelab/planisphere") library(planisphere) ## V8 engine 12.4.254.21 initialized ## Loading D3.js geospatial stack ## ✔ d3 (7.9.0) ## ✔ d3-geo-polygon (2.0.1) ## ✔ d3-geo-projection (4.0.0) ## ✔ d3-geo (3.1.1) ## ✔ additional script (spilhaus.js) ## Planisphere is ready 🌐 # Data Loading library(sf) ## Linking to GEOS 3.12.1, GDAL 3.8.4, PROJ 9.4.0; sf_use_s2() is TRUE world <- st_read(   system.file("gpkg/land.gpkg", package = "planisphere"),   quiet = TRUE ) result <- planisphere::project(x = world, proj = "geoInterruptedMollweide") planisphere::display(result) result <- planisphere::project(x = world, proj = "geoIcosahedral") |>    display()

title: Hello planisphere
text: Peirce projection   Peters (upside )   projection: d3.geoCylindricalEqualArea().parallel(45).rotate([167, 180]), Hao
code: peirceN <- planisphere::project(                             x = world,                             proj = "PeirceQuincuncial",                             rotate = c(-25, -90),                             additional_layers = TRUE                             ) display(peirceN) peirceS <- planisphere::project(                             x = world,                             proj = "PeirceQuincuncial",                             rotate = c(-25, 90, 180),                             additional_layers = TRUE                             ) display(peirceS) peters <- planisphere::project(                             x = world,                             proj = "geoCylindricalEqualArea",                             parallel = 45,                             rotate = c(167, 180),                             additional_layers = TRUE                             ) display(peters) polar <- planisphere::project(                             x = world,                             proj = "geoAzimuthalEquidistant",                             clipAngle = 150,                             rotate = c(0, -90),                              additional_layers = TRUE                             ) display(polar) hao <- planisphere::project(                             x = world,                             proj = "geoHufnagel",                             a = 0.8,                             b = 0.35,                             psiMax = 50,                             ratio = 1.6,                             angle = 90,                             rotate = c(110, -200, 90),                             additional_layers = TRUE                             ) display(hao)

title: Authors and Citation
text: Nicolas Lambert. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Lambert N (2026). planisphere: Map projections. R package version 0.1.0, https://neocarto.github.io/planisphere/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {planisphere: Map projections},   author = {Nicolas Lambert},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://neocarto.github.io/planisphere/}, }

title: Map projections
text: Map projections package provides access wide range cartographic projections. Built V8 engine, wraps D3.js libraries d3-geo, d3-geo-projection, d3-geo-polygon. Projection calculations performed using spherical geometry rather ellipsoidal geodetic models.

title: Map projections
text: package source code hosted GitHub can installed using remotes package.
code: install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("riatelab/planisphere")

title: Map projections
text: package provides three main functions. project() applies map projection geometries. display() renders projected result. new_v8_context() allow initialize new V8 engine custom JavaScript libraries. three operations can chained using pipe operator |>.
code: library(sf)  world <- st_read(   system.file("gpkg/land.gpkg", package = "planisphere"),   quiet = TRUE ) ct <- planisphere::init() result <- planisphere::project(ct, x = world, proj = "geoInterruptedMollweide") planisphere::display(result) planisphere::init() |>    planisphere::project(x = world, proj = "geoInterruptedMollweide") |>   planisphere::display()

what: What projection functions can I use?
title: Map projections
text: défault, projection functions available package provided three underlying JavaScript libraries. Please refer documentation libraries see available. d3-geo: https://d3js.org/d3-geo/projection d3-geo-projection: https://github.com/d3/d3-geo-projection d3-geo-polygon: https://github.com/d3/d3-geo-polygon Thanks Mike Bostock, Philippe Rivière, Jason Davies, Ricky Reusser, Charles Karney, contributors libraries worked developing spatial functions within D3.js ecosystem. example, package, can directly use "d3.geoRhombic()", simply "geoRhombic" "Rhombic". careful: uppercase lowercase letters important. projection functions configurable. example, obtain polar projection, can write:
code: planisphere::project(ct, x = world, proj = "d3.geoAzimuthalEquidistant().rotate([0, -90]).clipAngle(150)") planisphere::project(ct,                      x = world,                      proj = "AzimuthalEquidistant",                      rotate = c(0, -90),                      clipAngle = 150"                      )

what: Build a D3.js projection chain — build_projection_chain
title: Build a D3.js projection chain — build_projection_chain
text: Internal helper constructs JavaScript D3 projection call string.

title: Build a D3.js projection chain — build_projection_chain
code: build_projection_chain(proj = "geoSomething", verbose = TRUE, ...)

title: Build a D3.js projection chain — build_projection_chain
text: proj Character. Name D3 projection (without geo prefix). verbose Logical. TRUE, prints generated projection chain. ... Additional projection parameters passed method calls (e.g. rotate, center, scale, reflectX).

title: Build a D3.js projection chain — build_projection_chain
text: character string representing D3.js projection chain.

title: Build a D3.js projection chain — build_projection_chain
text: function translates R projection parameters valid D3.js projection constructor chain (e.g. d3.geoOrthographic().rotate([...])). used internally project() generate projection expression evaluated inside V8 JavaScript context.

title: Display a projected planisphere — display
text: Render complete planisphere projected object created project(). display includes projected sphere outline, graticule, geographic features.

title: Display a projected planisphere — display
code: display(x, title = NULL)

title: Display a projected planisphere — display
text: x list returned project(), containing three sf objects: basemap: projected geographic features sphere: outline projected globe graticule: projected latitude/longitude grid title Character string. Optional title add plot. Default NULL.

title: Display a projected planisphere — display
text: base R plot.

title: Display a projected planisphere — display
text: visualization follows D3.js spherical projection model used package. rendering order : 1. Sphere background 2. Graticule 3. Projected landmass (basemap) 4. Sphere outline geometries expected already projected spherical coordinates. function intended fast visualization exploration D3-based projections.

title: Display a projected planisphere — display
code: library(sf) #> Linking to GEOS 3.12.1, GDAL 3.8.4, PROJ 9.4.0; sf_use_s2() is TRUE world <- st_read(   system.file("gpkg/land.gpkg", package = "planisphere"),   quiet = TRUE )  result <- planisphere::project(x = world, proj = "geoInterruptedBoggs") #> Error in clean(x): object 'world' not found planisphere::display(result) #> Error: object 'result' not found

title: Initialize D3.js projection system — init
text: Create V8 JavaScript context load required D3.js libraries enable cartographic projections.

title: Initialize D3.js projection system — init
code: init(   libs = c("https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/d3@7", "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/d3-geo@3",     "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/d3-geo-projection@4",     "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/d3-geo-polygon@1"),   verbose = TRUE )

title: Initialize D3.js projection system — init
text: libs Character vector JavaScript library URLs load. default, libraries loaded jsDelivr CDN, URLs may replaced local files alternative CDNs. verbose Logical. TRUE, prints status loaded library console.

title: Initialize D3.js projection system — init
text: V8 JavaScript context specified libraries loaded.

title: Initialize D3.js projection system — init
text: function initializes V8 JavaScript runtime loads D3.js geospatial ecosystem used package, including: - d3 (core library) - d3-geo (spherical geometry projections) - d3-geo-projection (extended map projections) - d3-geo-polygon (polygon clipping sphere) Libraries loaded jsDelivr CDN default, local custom URLs can provided. projections package computed using D3.js spherical geometry, Earth modeled sphere rather ellipsoid.

title: Initialize D3.js projection system — init
code: ct <- planisphere::init() #> Loading JavaScript libraries #> ✔ https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/d3@7 #> ✔ https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/d3-geo@3 #> ✔ https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/d3-geo-projection@4 #> ✔ https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/d3-geo-polygon@1

title: New V8 context — new_v8_context
text: Create new V8 JavaScript context preloaded D3.js geospatial stack.

title: New V8 context — new_v8_context
code: new_v8_context(   libs = c("https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/d3@7", "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/d3-geo@3",     "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/d3-geo-polygon@2",     "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/d3-geo-projection@4"),   verbose = TRUE )

title: New V8 context — new_v8_context
text: libs Character vector JavaScript library URLs load. default, libraries loaded jsDelivr CDN. Users may replace local files, pinned versions, alternative CDNs. verbose Logical. TRUE, prints loading status library.

title: New V8 context — new_v8_context
text: V8 JavaScript context D3.js geospatial stack loaded ready cartographic computations.

title: New V8 context — new_v8_context
text: function initializes fresh V8 JavaScript runtime automatically loads latest versions required D3.js libraries jsDelivr CDN. following JavaScript libraries loaded default: - d3 (core library): https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/d3@7 - d3-geo (spherical geometry geographic projections): https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/d3-geo@3 - d3-geo-projection (extended cartographic projections): https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/d3-geo-projection@4 - d3-geo-polygon (spherical polygon clipping): https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/d3-geo-polygon@1 libraries fetched dynamically runtime jsDelivr CDN, ensuring recent compatible versions used unless explicitly overridden via `libs` argument. projections computed environment rely D3.js spherical geometry, Earth modeled sphere rather ellipsoid. allows fast consistent computation map projections geometric transformations.

title: New V8 context — new_v8_context
code: ct <- planisphere::new_v8_context() #> V8 engine 12.4.254.21 initialized #> Loading additional JavaScript libraries #> ✔ https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/d3@7 #> ✔ https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/d3-geo@3 #> ✔ https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/d3-geo-polygon@2 #> ✔ https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/d3-geo-projection@4 #> Planisphere is ready 🌐

what: Project geographic data using a D3.js projection — project
title: Project geographic data using a D3.js projection — project
text: Projects spatial data using D3.js geographic projections executed V8 JavaScript context. projection performed spherical coordinates, following D3.js design principles, Earth modeled sphere rather ellipsoid.

title: Project geographic data using a D3.js projection — project
code: project(   x,   proj = "geoAzimuthalEqualArea",   rotate = NULL,   reflectX = NULL,   reflectY = NULL,   scale = 500,   center = NULL,   clip = TRUE,   graticule = c(10, 10),   additional_layers = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE,   ct = .planisphere$ct,   ... )

title: Project geographic data using a D3.js projection — project
text: x sf spatial dataframe project. proj D3 projection name constructor (e.g. "geoInterruptedHomolosine"). rotate Rotation parameters passed projection. reflectX Logical; whether reflect projection X axis. reflectY Logical; whether reflect projection Y axis. scale Projection scale factor (D3 spherical scale, planar CRS units). center Optional projection center. clip TRUE, clips projected geometries projected sphere. graticule Numeric vector longitude/latitude step size graticule generation. ct custom V8 JavaScript context needed. See new_v8_context(). ... Additional parameters passed projection builder. additional_ayers Logical. TRUE, adds graticule sphere layers. case, function returns list. FALSE (default), returns spatial data frame.

title: Project geographic data using a D3.js projection — project
text: additionalLayers = FALSE, single sf object corresponding projected basemap. additionalLayers = TRUE, list sf objects: basemap: projected input geometries sphere: outline projected globe graticule: projected graticule lines

title: Project geographic data using a D3.js projection — project
text: D3 geographic projections based spherical geometry. transformations assume perfect sphere, differs traditional GIS pipelines often rely ellipsoidal datums (e.g., WGS84). result, function primarily intended visualization cartographic rendering rather high-precision geodetic computation.

title: Project geographic data using a D3.js projection — project
code: library(sf) world <- st_read(   system.file("gpkg/land.gpkg", package = "planisphere"),   quiet = TRUE )  result <- planisphere::project(x = world, proj = "geoInterruptedBoggs") #> Error in clean(x): object 'world' not found

title: List available projections — registry
text: Returns projections planisphere registry (d3-geo, d3-geo-projection, d3-geo-polygon).

title: List available projections — registry
code: registry(type = NULL)

title: List available projections — registry
text: type Projection family: "core", "continuous", "discontinuous", "quincuncial", "advanced"

title: List available projections — registry
text: character vector data.frame projection names.
